module_mood_contract: curious readiness — 'I've built enough to try; let's see what the agent does'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured at every teaching beat.
Epistemic mood — unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea, Key Concepts, and Start here all lead with positive disciplines: gap analysis, three-block frame, traces-are-data, every-send-off-is-an-experiment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running of M5 packaging teaching.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts line 37: 'Un-packaged is by design. Module 5 teaches packaging by diagnosing what the un-packaged run gets wrong, a lesson no lecture can land.' Names the intent without spoiling the contrast. No Ronacher three-pattern, plan.md, or verifier vocabulary appears in student-facing body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Six bullets cover themes: gap-analysis (compounding + capture-your-world), Huryn-frame (compounding), un-packaged-by-design (90%-correct + non-agentic-default), rules-files-in-test (non-agentic-default + capture-your-world), traces-are-data (90%-correct + compounding), experiment-not-production (compounding). Three or more themes present per rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — §§3/4/5 gates run first, breadcrumbs decorate survivors.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All three gates pass. Two 'Remember:' breadcrumbs present (lines 35, 39). Bullets 2, 4, 6 have no breadcrumb — candidates exist but non-presence is not a blocker.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three registry-marker fenced prompts (ae101-m4-commit-starting-point line 48, ae101-m4-take-task-end-to-end line 52, ae101-m4-nudge-continue line 63) carry the load-bearing mechanics. Body prose around them sets context only.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches work; no authored conversation or cohort-management cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'share with neighbor,' 'tell the room,' or pause-beat shapes found. 'The question, to you' (line 26) is work-directed address, not a cohort-management cue.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 130–132: (1) SHA-pinned branch before dispatch, falsifiable via git log; (2) stop stalled agent at ≤20 min and name failure mode in writing, falsifiable via scrollback; (3) walk memory+ADRs+skills, pick worst three gaps and write them, falsifiable via pre-task memory diff.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — contract row for every produced file.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 134–141: three-row table with stable identifier, producing prompt, and consuming module for each: m4/<task-slug> branch+SHA, filled gap entries in memory, M4 session transcript.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 143–152: seven-row table covering all forcing functions — Connections blocker, Phase 2 over-fill, Phase 2 gap-deferred-as-architectural (load-bearing M5-rescue gate), Phase 3 Huryn-as-lecture, Send-off SHA/branch loss, Send-off package-pre-empt, Send-off anxiety.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Test-and-learn lecture is an arming/conceptual opener (confirmed by reading lectures/test-and-learn.md: names experimental stance + gap analysis + Huryn frame BEFORE the student runs them — not a retrospective naming of a pattern already lived). Placement at Start here before the exercise is correct per rule 1. Reading-the-return is a bridge/pre-read placed at module close (line 65) — also correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4 is curious-readiness, not a contrast-mood module. Rule 2 targets M5-style contrast-mood. Not applicable.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Deliberate-antipattern prompts are class-judge exempt (flagged-by-design).
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m4-take-task-end-to-end is the un-packaged send-off prompt, deliberately under-packaged so M5's contrast lands. §51 carve-out applies. Behavior-class judge will flag it; story class treats as accepted-by-design.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Between-module reading appears at both ends of the module boundary.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4's '## Pre-reads before Module 5' (lines 77–85) carries M4's half of the symmetric pair with three readings and one-line framings. M5's Prework half cannot be verified from this file; this judge reads M4's half as present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands 8+ at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Single-phase module. Teaching beats: Start-here framing 8/10, Key-Concepts 8/10, Send-the-task-off section 8/10. Setup/navigation beats (lecture link, exercise link, Next section, Pre-reads) are mood-exempt per carve-out. Close score: 8. No beat below 7.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M4 doesn't pre-empt M5's contrast teaching.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 44: 'Compound on evidence, not anticipation.' Module explicitly defers the packaging diagnosis to M5. No Ronacher three-pattern names, no packaging prescription, no verifier vocabulary in student-facing body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The un-packaged send-off is the teaching moment and is encoded in ae101-m4-take-task-end-to-end (fenced, registry-expanded). Student cannot skip it without losing the module's sole deliverable. Prework callout (lines 10–12) and Bring-to-M5 callout (lines 73–75) both use the bring-or-scramble shape correctly, front-loading commitment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module honours the Big Idea contract.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea (line 4) names the four-beat arc: walk → fill → send un-packaged → learn from what the agent does. Module body maps exactly. Fourth beat (learn) is intentionally deferred to M5 — this is the §51 deliberate-antipattern design and strategy doc M4 intent, not a fidelity gap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — M4 close hands off cleanly to M5 open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 68–75: '## Next' and '## Bring to Module 5' both correctly frame M5 open ('Module 5 opens with what came back'). Artefact contract row in maintainer block names M4 transcript → M5 opener. Strategy doc M5 detail confirms 'M5 opens with the return.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal hand-off + Claude self-augments for send-off prompts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m4-take-task-end-to-end is the M4 walk-away send-off. Has (1) task reference, (2) rules+memory+ADRs+skills enumerated, (4) walk-away framing + report-back. Missing section (3): self-augment invitation — 'Before you start, ask for or write anything else you want for this run.' Strategy doc M4 detail explicitly describes this pattern as part of the send-off design. Without it, the prompt skips the Claude-self-augments step rule 19 defines as load-bearing for send-off prompts.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add one self-augment sentence inside the prompt fence, e.g.: 'Before you start, note anything you want to ask me, or write down anything you want for this run.' Requires maintainer approval per check_prompts §22.
